title: How Healthy Are Fish Pedicures?
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: In the past few years, fish pedicures have become very (0) fashionable. These (17) treatments involve a person placing their feet in a tank of water full of tiny Garra rufa fish. The fish nibble the person’s feet, eating and removing the dead skin. The benefits are said to be (18) numerous; the pedicures seem to stimulate blood flow and improve (19) circulation, they make the skin feel soft and clean, and they can help eliminate foot odour. Indeed, the (20) popularity of fish pedicures has exploded in the past few years and dozens of centres have opened nationwide.||However, there have been reports claiming that there is a (21) possibility that people could pick up an (22) infection from the water, the fish or other patients’ feet. Other experts claim the risk is small and that reports like these (23) overestimate the problem. However, they do recommend that centres undergo regular inspections to make sure the pedicures are not being carried out in (24) unhygienic conditions.

example.text: In the past few years, fish pedicures have become very (0) . FASHION

questions.explanation: Se necesita un sustantivo plural que describa las sesiones de pedicura.
Adjetivo que significa 'muchos' o 'en gran número'.
Sustantivo que se refiere al flujo sanguíneo.
Sustantivo que expresa el grado de aceptación general.
Sustantivo que expresa la existencia de un riesgo potencial.
Sustantivo que nombra la enfermedad que se puede contraer.
Verbo con prefijo que significa valorar algo por encima de lo real.
Adjetivo con prefijo negativo que indica malas condiciones de higiene.
